story.description: A clip of plants, leaves, flowers, or plant movement: close up green leaf with warm background
story.hook: Plants turn light into stored sugar.
story.lead: Plants turn light into stored sugar. Watch the leaf surface first, because that detail shows the mechanism in motion. The source moment is close up green leaf warm background. Chlorophyll catches energy so the leaf can build sugar from air and water. By the last line, the opening detail has a job viewers can name.

story.description: A clip of cold-climate animals: elephant seals resting on sandy beach
story.hook: Seals can follow wakes with their whiskers.
story.lead: Seals can follow wakes with their whiskers. Watch the whiskers near the water, because seals can sense tiny trails left by moving fish. Those vibrations help them hunt even when the water is dark or messy. The face becomes a current detector. Which ocean sense next?

story.description: A clip of farm animals: horses near tree
story.lead: Horses can remember familiar faces. Watch the eyes and ears, because horses can learn who is familiar and who feels safe. That memory changes how calmly they approach people and other horses. The look is part recognition, part decision. Which herd animal next?

story.description: A clip of dogs / puppies: dogs lying in front of a laptop
story.hook: Dogs can follow a human point fast.
story.lead: Dogs can follow a human point fast. Watch the eyes check the person, because dogs are unusually good at using human gestures. That skill helps them cooperate with us before any command gets repeated. The small glance is social intelligence, not random obedience. What dog skill should come next?
story.script: Dogs can follow a human point fast. Watch the eyes check the person, because dogs are unusually good at using human gestures. That skill helps them cooperate with us before any command gets repeated. The small glance is social intelligence, not random obedience. What dog skill should come next?

story.hook: Coral reefs are built by tiny living builders.
story.lead: Coral reefs are built by tiny living builders. Watch the reef texture, because coral animals build hard skeletons that stack into habitat. Fish, algae, and countless small creatures then use that structure like a city. The color sits on top of engineering. Which habitat next?

story.hook: Plants turn light into stored sugar.
story.lead: Plants turn light into stored sugar. Watch the leaf surface first, because that detail shows the mechanism in motion. The source moment is a plants on a farm. Chlorophyll catches energy so the leaf can build sugar from air and water. By the last line, the opening detail has a job viewers can name.

story.description: A clip of dogs / puppies: a man is walking on the beach with his dog
story.hook: Dogs build a map with smell.
story.lead: Dogs build a map with smell. Watch the nose before the feet, because every sniff samples layers of scent. Dogs can learn who passed, where they moved, and what changed since the last check. The ground is basically a news feed. What should they sniff next?

reasons: script does not expose enough concrete visual detail
